question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 63.00% on 2026-03-11 for the Metaculus question "Will Keir Starmer cease to be Prime Minister of the UK during 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether Metaculus CP for "Will Keir Starmer cease to be PM during 2026?" will exceed 63% on March 11. The CP was at exactly 63% as of March 1 (from question metadata). Key evidence: Manifold market for the same underlying event is at 66-67% with an upward trend, suggesting cross-platform forecasters perceive higher probability than the current Metaculus CP. Continuing negative news (by-election loss, minister resignations, Iran crisis) create upward pressure. However, Starmer survived the February leadership challenge, is actively governing, and the large 783-forecaster base creates CP inertia. On balance, evidence mildly favors the CP moving above 63%, primarily driven by the cross-platform signal from Manifold.

factors.description: CP is stated as 63.00% as of March 1 in question metadata (last_cp: 0.63). Right at the threshold - any net upward movement resolves YES.

Manifold market for underlying event at 66-67% with upward trend (60.2% Feb 24 to 67% Mar 1). Cross-platform signal suggests perceived probability is above the 63% threshold, creating upward pressure on Metaculus CP.
Continuing negative news not fully priced into Mar 1 CP: Labour lost Gorton & Denton by-election to Greens (Feb 27), minister resigned (Josh Simons, Mar 1), approval at historic lows.
Major Iran crisis (UK base in Cyprus attacked Mar 2, UK allows US to use bases Mar 1, Trump criticizes Starmer Mar 3) adds political instability and forecaster concern.
Starmer survived February leadership challenge and is actively governing (appointing new ministers Mar 3, making Iran policy). No imminent resignation from credible sources.
CP inertia: 783 forecasters means large base harder to move. CP already absorbed major negative events (Epstein scandal, February challenge) without moving far above 63%.
William Hill betting odds favor 2026 as Starmer exit year, supporting directional pressure for CP to rise over time.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.60 (60% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:
- The Metaculus CP was at exactly 63.00% as of March 1 — right at the threshold where any upward movement triggers YES
- The Manifold market for the same underlying question is at 66-67% with an upward trend, suggesting cross-platform forecasters see the probability as higher than 63%
- Recent events (by-election loss, minister resignation, Iran crisis with UK base attacked) create additional upward pressure on the CP
- However, Starmer survived the February leadership challenge, is actively governing, and the large forecaster base (783) creates inertia that could keep CP anchored near 63%
- Net assessment: mild upward pressure favors CP exceeding 63% by March 11, but not strongly directional given CP resistance to moving much higher despite significant negative events
